questions.subject: Engineering & Environmental Geology
questions.stem: Rock Quality Designation (RQD) is computed from drill core as the percentage of
options: intact pieces ≥10 cm summed over the run length
questions.solution:
**Concept.** RQD = Σ(intact pieces ≥10 cm) / total core run × 100.

**Working.**
Pieces ≥10 cm.

**Answer.** Σ pieces ≥10 cm

questions.subject: Engineering & Environmental Geology
questions.stem: A rock joint has cohesion $30\,$kPa and friction angle $30^\circ$. Under a normal stress of $100\,$kPa, its shear strength is _____ kPa.

questions.subject: Engineering & Environmental Geology
questions.stem: A planar rock slope is most prone to sliding when the discontinuity
options: daylights and dips less steeply than the slope face
is vertical and parallel to the slope
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Plane failure needs a discontinuity that daylights and is flatter than the face but steeper than friction.

**Working.**
Daylighting, flatter than face.

**Answer.** Daylights, flatter than slope

questions.subject: Engineering & Environmental Geology
questions.stem: Which are recognised triggers of landslides?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Rainfall, seismicity and toe undercutting trigger slides; reducing slope height improves stability.

**Working.**
Options 1, 2, 3.

**Answer.** Landslide triggers

questions.subject: Engineering & Environmental Geology
questions.stem: An aquifer that is fully saturated and confined between two aquitards is best described as
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Confined aquifers are bounded by aquitards and are under pressure (artesian).

**Working.**
Confined.

**Answer.** Confined aquifer
